etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + 尔(爾) (ěr, phonetic) = related to wooden object (loom)
etymology.interpretation: 檷(nǐ, "loom") combines the 木(木) (mù, "wood") radical, hinting at a wooden object, with the phonetic 尔(爾) (ěr), which gives a clue to its pronunciation. The character refers to a traditional loom, a wooden frame used for weaving.
